title: Samsung Profits Surge as AI Chip Demand Hits Record Levels
subtitle: The technology giant reports a massive jump in earnings, reflecting the global race to build artificial intelligence infrastructure.

summary.en: Samsung Electronics has announced a nineteen-fold increase in its quarterly profits. This growth is driven by the high demand for memory chips used in artificial intelligence. While the company is performing well, some investors felt the results did not meet their high expectations. Experts believe that the supply of these chips will remain limited for some time. Meanwhile, South Korea is investing heavily to maintain its lead in the global semiconductor industry.
summary.zh: 三星電子宣布其季度獲利成長十九倍。這波成長是由人工智慧所需的記憶體晶片高需求所驅動。儘管公司表現亮眼，部分投資人認為結果未達其高標準的預期。專家認為，這些晶片的供應在一段時間內仍將維持緊繃。與此同時，南韓正投入鉅資以維持其在全球半導體產業的領先地位。

paragraphs.en: Samsung Electronics, the South Korean technology giant, has announced a massive increase in its profits, reporting a 19-fold jump compared to previous periods. This record-breaking performance is largely driven by the global need for specialized memory chips used in artificial intelligence (AI). According to BBC Business, the company expects to report 89.4 trillion won in operating profits for the second quarter of the year. This marks the third time in a row that the company has achieved record quarterly profits.
paragraphs.zh: 南韓科技巨頭三星電子（Samsung Electronics）宣布獲利大幅成長，與過往同期相比暴增了十九倍。這項破紀錄的表現，主要歸功於全球對人工智慧（AI）專用記憶體晶片的龐大需求。根據英國廣播公司商業新聞（BBC Business）報導，該公司預計今年第二季的營業利益將達到八十九點四兆韓元。這已是三星連續第三季創下獲利新高紀錄。

paragraphs.en: This update follows recent reports on how the AI boom is changing the global economy. As noted in earlier coverage, the demand for semiconductors—the tiny electronic parts that act as the 'brains' for computers and phones—is currently higher than the available supply. Because companies around the world are racing to build AI data centers, they need more memory chips than ever before. This high demand has allowed Samsung to increase the prices of its products, which has significantly boosted its financial results.
paragraphs.zh: 這項最新消息反映了近期人工智慧熱潮如何改變全球經濟。正如先前報導所指出的，半導體（即作為電腦與手機「大腦」的微小電子零件）的需求目前已供不應求。由於全球企業競相建置人工智慧資料中心，對記憶體晶片的需求達到前所未有的程度。這種強勁的需求讓三星得以調漲產品價格，進而顯著提升了財務表現。

paragraphs.en: Industry experts suggest that this trend is not slowing down. Marc Einstein, an analyst from Counterpoint Research, described the situation as a 'tidal wave' of demand. He noted that the company is experiencing one of the best performances in its history, similar to the success seen by other major tech firms like Nvidia earlier this year. However, the market reaction was mixed. Despite the strong growth, BBC Business reports that Samsung’s shares fell by almost 7% on the Seoul stock market. Some investors had hoped for even higher profits, showing that expectations for tech companies remain extremely high.
paragraphs.zh: 產業專家認為這股趨勢並未放緩。市場研究機構 Counterpoint Research 的分析師愛因斯坦（Marc Einstein）形容這種情況為需求「如潮水般湧來」。他指出，該公司正經歷史上表現最好的時期之一，與輝達（Nvidia）等其他大型科技公司今年稍早的成功表現相似。然而，市場反應卻好壞參半。儘管成長強勁，BBC 商業新聞報導指出，三星在首爾股市的股價仍下跌了近百分之七。部分投資人原本期待更高的獲利，這顯示市場對科技公司的期望值依然極高。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the challenge for the industry will be managing the limited supply of these essential components. Bryan Ma, a researcher from IDC, explained that the demand for chips for AI data centers is unlike anything the memory industry has seen before. He expects that supplies will remain tight through next year because the need for these chips continues to grow without stopping. This shortage affects more than just AI; it also impacts the supply of chips available for everyday electronics, such as smartphones and home appliances.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，產業面臨的挑戰將是如何管理這些關鍵零組件的有限供應。國際數據資訊（IDC）的研究員馬布萊恩（Bryan Ma）解釋說，人工智慧資料中心對晶片的需求，是記憶體產業前所未見的。他預期供應短缺的情況將持續到明年，因為對這些晶片的需求仍不斷攀升。這種短缺不僅影響人工智慧領域，也波及智慧型手機與家電等日常電子產品的晶片供應。

paragraphs.en: Samsung remains one of the world’s most important manufacturers. It produces chips for major global firms like Google and Nvidia, while also making its own range of electronic devices. The success of Samsung and its local rival, SK Hynix, has had a positive effect on the South Korean economy. The value of the country’s main share index, the Kospi, has risen by more than 80% this year, largely thanks to the strong performance of these semiconductor companies.
paragraphs.zh: 三星依然是全球最重要的製造商之一。它不僅為 Google 與輝達等全球大型企業生產晶片，同時也製造自家的一系列電子裝置。三星及其在地競爭對手 SK 海力士（SK Hynix）的成功，對南韓經濟產生了正面影響。南韓主要股價指數「綜合股價指數」（Kospi）今年已上漲超過百分之八十，這很大程度上要歸功於這些半導體公司的強勁表現。

paragraphs.en: To keep this momentum going, the South Korean government is taking action. In June, the country announced plans to invest at least $880 billion into projects led by Samsung and SK Hynix. This investment is intended to help build more chip manufacturing factories, ensuring that the country can continue to meet the rising global demand. This is part of a wider trend, as rival firms in Japan, China, and Taiwan are also spending large amounts of money to expand their own production capabilities.
paragraphs.zh: 為了延續這股動能，南韓政府正採取行動。該國在六月宣布計畫，將投入至少八千八百億美元，支持由三星與 SK 海力士主導的專案。這項投資旨在協助興建更多晶片製造廠，確保南韓能持續滿足全球日益增長的需求。這也是全球趨勢的一部分，因為日本、中國與台灣的競爭對手也正投入鉅資，以擴大各自的生產能力。

paragraphs.en: While the current situation is very positive for chip makers, the market remains sensitive. When Nvidia reported record sales earlier this year, its stock price also fell, which analysts interpreted as a sign that investors are worried about future competition. As more companies enter the market and invest in their own production, the landscape of the semiconductor industry will likely continue to change. For now, however, the AI boom continues to be the primary force driving growth for companies like Samsung, as they work to balance limited supply with the massive needs of the modern digital world.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然目前的局勢對晶片製造商非常有利，但市場依然敏感。當輝達今年稍早公布創紀錄的銷售額時，其股價也曾下跌，分析師將此解讀為投資人對未來競爭感到擔憂的跡象。隨著更多企業進入市場並投資自有產能，半導體產業的版圖很可能會持續變動。然而就目前而言，人工智慧熱潮仍是推動三星等公司成長的主要動力，因為他們正努力在有限的供應與現代數位世界龐大的需求之間取得平衡。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A sudden, large increase in amount or level.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（跳躍），這裡作名詞用。文中：Samsung Electronics, the South Korean technology giant, has announced a massive increase in its profits, reporting a 19-fold jump compared to previous periods.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Limited in quantity; not enough to meet demand.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指「緊的」（如繩子），這裡指供應不足。文中：He expects that supplies will remain tight through next year because the need for these chips continues to grow without stopping.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Containing both positive and negative elements; not entirely good or bad.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 好壞參半的、反應不一的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（混合），這裡作形容詞描述市場反應。文中：However, the market reaction was mixed.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Samsung profits jump 1,800% as AI chip sales soar

previousArticles.title: The AI Boom: How Memory Chips Are Changing the Global Economy
previousArticles.summaryEn: Global demand for artificial intelligence technology is driving record profits for major semiconductor companies. Samsung Electronics recently reported a massive increase in earnings due to the high need for specialized memory chips. Analysts suggest that this trend is fueled by the rapid growth of AI data centers worldwide. While the industry is seeing unprecedented success, experts warn that supply shortages may continue for some time. Countries across Asia are now investing heavily in local manufacturing to keep up with this competitive market.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary reason for the recent decline in Samsung's share price despite its record-breaking profits?

options.A: The company failed to meet the production targets set by the South Korean government.
options.B: Investors had anticipated even higher financial gains than what was actually reported.
options.C: Increased competition from rival firms in Japan and China has weakened investor confidence.
options.D: The global demand for memory chips has started to decrease unexpectedly.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，儘管三星獲利創下紀錄，但股價下跌了近 7%，原因是投資人對科技公司的期待極高，原本希望看到比實際報告更高的獲利。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 C 雖然提到其他國家在擴張產能，但並非導致此次股價下跌的主因；選項 D 則與文章提到的「需求持續成長」矛盾。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the impact of the AI boom on consumers of everyday electronics like smartphones?

options.A: They will likely benefit from cheaper prices due to mass production of chips.
options.B: They may face difficulties or delays in purchasing devices due to component shortages.
options.C: They will see a significant improvement in the quality of their home appliances.
options.D: They will be required to pay higher taxes to fund the government's chip investment projects.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段，作者提到 AI 資料中心對晶片的需求龐大，導致供應緊縮，而這種短缺不只影響 AI，還會影響智慧型手機和家電等日常電子產品的晶片供應。由此可推論，供應不足可能導致這些產品出現短缺或取得困難。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 文中並未提及產品品質提升；選項 D 則屬於過度推論，文中未提及稅收問題。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the phrase 'tidal wave' suggest about the demand for memory chips?

options.A: The demand is rising steadily and predictably over a long period.
options.B: The demand is destructive and causing long-term damage to the market.
options.C: The demand is overwhelming, sudden, and extremely powerful.
options.D: The demand is fluctuating and difficult for analysts to measure accurately.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文中分析師 Marc Einstein 使用「tidal wave」（海嘯）來形容需求，海嘯在比喻中通常代表規模巨大、來勢洶洶且難以阻擋的現象，符合文中描述 AI 晶片需求極高的情境。選項 A 的「平穩」與海嘯的猛烈特質不符；選項 B 的「破壞性」雖有負面意涵，但文中強調的是獲利與成長，而非市場毀滅；選項 D 的「波動」則忽略了該詞強調的「巨大規模」。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding Samsung's current situation?

options.A: Samsung is shifting its focus away from memory chips to prioritize smartphone production.
options.B: The South Korean government is taking over the management of private tech companies.
options.C: The AI boom is driving record profits for Samsung, but it also creates supply challenges and high market expectations.
options.D: Global competition is causing Samsung to lose its status as the world's leading chip manufacturer.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文整體論述了 AI 熱潮如何為三星帶來創紀錄的獲利，同時也討論了隨之而來的供應鏈挑戰（第四段）、市場對其高期待導致的股價波動（第三、七段），以及政府為維持競爭力所做的投資（第六段）。選項 A 與文意不符；選項 B 誇大了政府的角色；選項 D 則與文中強調三星仍處於市場重要地位的敘述不符。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
